channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Your phone got hacked and now someone's got control of it. What to do immediately
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson lays out the immediate steps you should take if your phone has been hacked and your personal information becomes vulnerable.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How you can prepare your TikTok account for the upcoming ban
item.description: If you’re one of the 170 million TikTok users in the U.S., it’s time to start making sure your account is properly secured and updated ahead of the impending January ban.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Court denies TikTok's request to halt enforcement of potential US ban until Supreme Court review
item.description: A federal appeals court on Friday left in place a mid-January deadline in a federal law requiring TikTok to be sold or face a ban in the United States, rejecting a request made by the company to halt enforcement until the Supreme Court reviews its challenge of the statute.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Meta wants Threads to be more like Bluesky. Again
item.description: The surest sign a social media platform is winning: when Meta starts cribbing notes from it. Stories only arrived on Instagram in 2016 after Snapchat proved the ephemeral concept had some heat.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Groups urge Trump to reinforce National Cyber Director role
item.description: The role of the White House national cyber director has emerged as a key issue for former cyber officials as the Trump administration prepares to take over. The post Groups urge Trump to reinforce National Cyber Director role first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: For December’s Patch Tuesday, 74 updates and a zero-day fix for Windows
item.description: Microsoft released 74 updates in its December Patch Tuesday update, with patches for Windows, Office and Edge — but none for Microsoft Exchange Server or SQL server.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Warning: This fake PayPal email scam is everywhere right now
item.description: Fraudsters are sending people bogus invoices through PayPal as part of a sneaky scam that is going around; here's how to protect yourself from being fooled.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As AI makes its way into movies, Duncan Crabtree-Ireland is fighting for actors’ rights
item.description: After years of advocating for actors’ collective rights, Duncan Crabtree-Ireland is finally getting his own close-up.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Snow, Fast Company

item.title: Sophia, a famous robot and global icon of AI, wins hearts at Zimbabwe's innovation fair
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — From answering questions from Cabinet ministers, academics and students on climate change, substance abuse and the law to children’s inquiries about her “birth” and links to God and being described as a talkative feminist, Sophia, the world-famous robot won hearts at an innov
item.dc:creator: BY FARAI MUTSAKA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: OpenAI's Altman will donate $1 million to Trump's inaugural fund
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman is planning to make a $1 million personal donation to President-Elect Donald Trump’s inauguration fund, joining a number of tech companies and executives who are working to improve their relationships the incoming administration.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Google CEO eyes AI 'Manhattan Project' after Trump inauguration
item.description: Alphabet and Google CEO Sundar Pichai said in a recent interview that he is eyeing a research project on artificial intelligence (AI), similar to the Manhattan Project during World War II, after President-elect Trump returns to the White House next month.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Feds propose protection for giant salamanders devastated by Hurricane Helene
item.description: A giant salamander that evolved on the supercontinent Pangaea and outlived the dinosaurs has been proposed for federal protection by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Peterson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Process Intelligence: The CIO’s secret weapon for unlocking value
item.description: CIOs are under intense pressure to deliver massive digital transformation initiatives with limited resources under tight time constraints. Boards of directors are placing a high priority on deploying generative AI as fast as possible so their organizations don’t lose competitive advantage.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Metaphor: ReFantazio, Dragon Age, Astro Bot and an indie wave lead the top AP video games of 2024
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s been a rough year in the world of video games, which reeled from some high-profile flops (sorry, Suicide Squad and Star Wars fans) and a relentless series of developer layoffs. Fortunately, indie designers stepped up to fill in the gaps.
item.dc:creator: LOU KESTEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US updates a science and technology pact with China to reflect growing rivalry and security threats
item.description: The U.S. has updated a decades-old science and technology agreement with China to reflect their growing rivalry for technological dominance.
item.dc:creator: Didi Tang, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Discover’s hybrid cloud journey pays off
item.description: Discover Financial Services has moved aggressively to the cloud in 2024 with a migration strategy focused on retaining hybrid flexibility and making the most of cloud elasticity.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Gig workers behind AI training firm sue for better conditions
item.description: A new class action lawsuit alleges poor working conditions and exploitive behavior by AI data processing company Scale AI, saying that workers responsible for generating much of its product were mischaracterized by the company as independent contractors, rather than full employees.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: DoorDash steps up driver ID checks after traffic safety complaints
item.description: DoorDash will require its drivers to verify their identities more often as part of a larger effort to crack down on unauthorized account sharing. DoorDash has been under pressure to ensure its drivers are operating legally.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No support or updates for Windows 11 on machines not meeting minimum hardware requirements, says Microsoft
item.description: Microsoft has offered a miniscule concession to users determined to install Windows 11 on PCs that don’t meet its minimum hardware requirements: you will be able to do it, but on your own head be it should things go wrong.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Kazakhstan’s Carpet CCTV: Pioneering the future of AI-powered public safety
item.description: In a world where technology increasingly shapes how cities manage safety and security, Kazakhstan’s Ministry of Internal Affairs is leading the way with its groundbreaking “Carpet CCTV” project.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Mozilla is removing Firefox’s anti-tracking function
item.description: Mozilla has announced that Do Not Track, a feature designed to prevent user tracking online, will be removed with version 135 of the company’s Firefox browser. The decision comes as more and more websites choose to ignore Do Not Track, leaving users with a false sense of security.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple Intelligence arrives for the UK, Australia, Canada, New Zealand with iOS 18.2
item.description: Apple has released iOS 18.2, iPadOS 18.2, and macOS Sequoia 15.2, which means local language access to Apple Intelligence is now available to iPhone, Mac, and iPad users in the UK, Australia, Canada, New Zealand.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The makers and takers of WordPress
item.description: The idea of open-source software seems kind of nuts. Millions (billions?) of lines of code doing all kinds of amazing things and available for free? That sounds too good to be true. But it is true.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Fortnite players 'tricked' into unwanted purchases are starting to get refunds. Here's how to apply
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. consumers who were “tricked” into purchases they didn't want from Fortnite maker Epic Games are now starting to receive refund checks, the Federal Trade Commission said this week.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nokia expands data center SONiC support
item.description: Nokia is offering customers an alternative way to set up data center networks by expanding its data center fabric package to support the open-source Software for Open Networking in the Cloud (SONiC).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Databricks unveils synthetic data generation API to help evaluate agents faster
item.description: As enterprise demand for building multi-agent systems continues to grow, data infrastructure services providing firm, Databricks, is updating its Mosaic AI Agent Evaluation module with a new synthetic data generation API that will help enterprises evaluate agents faster.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia's stock dips after China opens probe of the AI chip company for violating anti-monopoly laws
item.description: Shares of Nvidia fell Monday after China said it is investigating the high-flying U.S. microchip company over suspected violations of Chinese anti-monopoly laws.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California to consider requiring mental health warnings on social media sites
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California, home to some of the largest technology companies in the world, would be the first U.S. state to require mental health warning labels on social media sites if lawmakers pass a bill introduced Monday.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, NewsNation
